index.description: L2 index of methodologies in the research domain. The retriever reads this after picking research from L1 domains.xml. Entries list slug + tier + summary; open the leaf meta.json / content/*.xml for full payload.

methodology.summary: Cluster atomic interview insights into named themes via affinity diagramming — produces a labelled cluster board with size + confidence per cluster.

Revenue-mix audit template that classifies an agency's last-12-months revenue by type (project, retainer, productized, referral, owner-billed) and surfaces dependence risks + valuation-multiplier impact.

Weighted 1-5 rubric that converts qualitative agency-quality signals (productized revenue %, retainer %, owner-dependency, niche stickiness) into a comparable 0-100 valuation multiplier on top of EBITDA.
Config spec for invoking research subagents safely: bounded tool whitelist, structured input schema, structured output schema, max-iterations cap, and observable trace contract.
Builds research-grounded personas from interview transcripts + behavioral data using staged LLM passes with explicit segment criteria and human verification.
Protocol for AI-assisted qualitative coding (codebook design, inter-coder agreement, audit trail) combining LLM proposal with human reconciliation to reach publishable kappa.
Defines instrumentable trust metrics for an AI feature (perceived accuracy, transparency, recovery, fairness) and how to measure them via in-product probes + survey deltas.
Analyzes a single interview transcript into a structured artefact (highlights, codes, contradictions, follow-up questions) with quoted evidence + researcher verification.
Builds research-grounded personas from interview transcripts + behavioral data using staged LLM passes with explicit segment criteria and human verification.
Taxonomy of AI research tool categories (live-web search, citation graphs, document Q&A, synthesis-over-uploaded-sources) with selection criteria and corpus-bias notes per category.
Selection map of concrete AI research tools (Perplexity, Elicit, Consensus, Scite, NotebookLM) matched to research-task shape, evidence requirements, and budget.
Codified user-research protocol that turns the post-MVP PMF hunt into a repeatable, auditable artefact biased toward churn-segment power users.

5-step audience-segmentation report partitioning a market into 2-5 scored segments along 2-3 dimensions (demographic, behavioural, psychographic, needs-based) with target-segment decision and ICP profile.
Recruits a private-beta / design-partner cohort with NDA, expectations contract, feedback cadence, and exit ramp — distinct from open user-interview recruiting.

Four-framework spec (5-criteria weighted niche viability, Blue Ocean Four Actions, Value Proposition Canvas, pricing-model selection) that ranks 3-5 niches in parallel and outputs a monetization decision.
Builds a Business Model Canvas (9 blocks, Hard/Soft tagged) with P10/P50/P90 unit economics and 5 stress tests, ending in a viable / viable-with-fixes / not-viable verdict.
Comparative research report mapping 5-10 business models against the candidate product economics (CAC, LTV, gross margin, churn) and ranking them by fit, not by industry default.
Mom-Test-style buyer interview script tuned for freelancers selling a service offer — hiring triggers, budget authority, vendor preference, switching pain.
Continuous CI pipeline that splits mechanical collection (Haiku) from strategic synthesis (Opus); produces dated weekly digests, monthly threat assessments, and battlecards with a hard 14-day TTL.
Ongoing competitor-monitoring report that ingests pricing pages, product changelogs, hiring posts, and review-site movement into a structured CI brief with cited deltas vs prior cycle.

Catalogue of 8-12 named CI collection techniques (review-site scrape, pricing-page diff, hiring-signal, patent-pull, GitHub-velocity, customer-call probe) with applicability rules and known false-signal modes.

5-step framework (identify -> map -> analyse -> gap -> differentiate) producing competitive-analysis.md across direct/indirect/future competitors on 8 dimensions, ending in a sourced positioning statement.
Structured competitor analysis report comparing 3-7 direct + 2-4 indirect competitors across pricing, positioning, distribution, GTM motion, and review-site sentiment with cited evidence per cell.
Monthly competitor matrix + weekly scan schema (channels watched, last delta, action queued) so competitor signal doesn't devolve into noise.

Teresa Torres weekly-rhythm discovery: 6-cadence pipeline (daily signals -> weekly interviews + competitor + assumption tests -> bi-weekly OST -> monthly review) with N>=5 evidence floor and 8 scheduled subagents.

Weekly continuous-discovery operating cadence: opportunity-solution-tree maintenance, \u22653 customer touches/week, signal pipeline (interview + behavioural + market) with raw_hash + source_url discipline.
Bullseye-style channel evaluation: shortlist 3 of 19 channels, run small-budget tests (<=$500 each), score on CAC + reach + time-to-signal, lock the top 1-2 channels with stop-loss tripwires.

Distribution-channel report scoring 5-8 channels (SEO, paid, partnerships, marketplaces, communities, outbound, content, referral) by CAC, LTV-fit, and time-to-first-revenue with cited benchmarks per channel.
Systematically discover, prioritize, and validate product features before engineering — produces a RICE-scored feature-discovery board feeding the roadmap.
Router methodology that takes a research goal and routes the agent to the correct framework (TAM/SAM/SOM, Five Forces, JTBD, OST, Lean Canvas, Kano) instead of mixing them.
Researcher-tier ideation: 7 frameworks (skills, pain mining, job substitution, productized service, unbundling, market stacking, own problems) producing 20–50 scored candidates.
Bundle of four complementary pre-validation methods (7P framework, PG four questions, pain-point mining, 5-dimension niche matrix) producing a scored candidate list for downstream validation.
Pins a single interview insight to a fixed card (claim + evidence count + quote + segment) so synthesis decays into reviewable evidence, not opinion.
5-bullet hot-take capture within 30 minutes of an interview — pins the freshness signal before formal synthesis flattens it.
Pins a controlled tag vocabulary for atomic-insight tagging so interview synthesis feeds an opportunity tree, not a tag-soup.
Synthesizes raw user-interview notes into a structured insight report with quotes, themes, and confidence ratings using staged LLM passes + human verification.
Map customer progress via switching interviews and tag Push / Pull / Habit / Fear forces — produces a job-map + force-aggregation report.
Switch / Forces-of-Progress interview protocol that surfaces push, pull, anxiety, habit forces driving real buyer switches; produces a 4-force scored report anchored to verbatim transcripts.
Market-analysis report covering segments, sizing, growth, regulation, key buyers, key sellers, and 3-5 PEST/STEEP-level forces shaping the market over a 12-24 month horizon.
Triangulates TAM/SAM/SOM with both top-down (industry report scaled) and bottom-up (price x reachable units) numbers; demands both to agree within 30% and refuses single-source figures.
Market-sizing report computing TAM, SAM, and SOM via both top-down and bottom-up methods with cited unit counts \u00d7 pricing and an explicit reconciliation when the two methods disagree by >2x.
Three-method market sizing (top-down, bottom-up, value-theory) using AI tools for source gathering with citation-backed estimates and reconciliation across the three methods.
Produces a deep-dive page per research methodology (when-to-use, core-rules, output-contract, anti-patterns, validators) for use inside the faion.net knowledge surface.
Authors the L2 INDEX.xml for the research domain: groups methodologies into 3-8 sub-clusters, attaches complexity + produces + summary, keeps the list sorted and count-correct.
Triangulation report cross-checking findings across \u22653 independent methods (qualitative interviews + quantitative survey + behavioural log) with an explicit convergence/divergence map per claim.
Generate product names against trademark / pronounceability / SEO constraints, then validate domain + handle availability before launch.
Researcher-tier niche evaluation: definition + TAM/SAM/SOM + competitor matrix + audience + monetization + fit, biased to research-anchored inputs over sizing-by-feel.
Six-step framework producing a niche-evaluation report: niche one-sentence formula, TAM/SAM/SOM with cited sources, competitor matrix, audience accessibility, monetization, and personal fit.
5-criteria weighted niche scorecard (Market Size 25%, Competition 20%, Barriers 20%, Profitability 20%, Your Fit 15%) scoring 3-5 niches in parallel with cited sources per criterion.
Builds and maintains a Torres-style OST with one outcome at the root, opportunities scored on freq x sev x addressability, and solutions branching only off opportunities with falsifiable assumptions.
Systematically discover and score customer pain points across public sources (Reddit, G2, App Store, Quora, Upwork) — produces a scored pain-point log feeding ideation.
Decompose-execute-synthesize Perplexity Pro Search workflow: atomic sub-queries, recency filters, per-claim confidence ratings, and human verification before publication.
Produces 3 personas (primary + secondary + negative), each backed by >=5 interview citations, JTBD statements, top-3 pains, and a kill-criteria block; refuses single-source personas.
Researcher-tier pricing research combining value-capture math, competitor matrix, Van Westendorp WTP, billing model, and tier design — anchored to research evidence.
Six-step pricing report: value math, competitor matrix, Van Westendorp 4-question survey, billing model choice, tier design, and validation via real sales.
Confirm a problem is real, painful, and worth solving via Mom-Test-style interviews — produces a validation report with GO / PASS / PIVOT verdict before code is written.

Produces a quarterly problem-validation report (evidence ledger sorted on 5-level hierarchy + cold-respondent count + validated-or-hypothesis verdict) so problems are pinned to behavioral signals, not stated preferences.
Scores emerging trends on a 4-axis rubric (signal strength + adoption velocity + revenue alignment + decay risk) producing a quarterly trend brief with explicit 'bet / monitor / ignore' verdicts.

Snapshot specialisation of trend research locked to 2026 signals (on-device LLM, agent-as-product, vibe-coding, privacy-first, sustainability), with hard freshness cutoff (no sources older than 18 months).
2026-vintage trend scan over product-development practice with valid_until dates per item; auto-expires when a trend's underlying signal goes stale or shifts a hype-curve phase.
Trend-scan report for product-development practice (tooling, methodologies, dev-loop changes) with cited adoption signals, hype-curve placement, and applicability notes per trend.
Produces a research-repo filename + folder spec (slug grammar + manifest.json schema + lifecycle folders) so AI-assisted synthesis can locate, group, and cite artefacts deterministically.
Day-2 ops for a research repository: ingestion, tagging, deduplication, retention, access control, weekly health report, and a kill-switch for stale studies.
Initial setup of a research repository (Dovetail / Notion / Airtable): platform selection, taxonomy authoring, access matrix, ingestion wiring, kickoff backfill of last 90 days.
Builds a 1-page risk register: top 7 risks with P10/P50/P90 likelihood, severity, mitigation owner, trigger, and a decision gate; rejects vague 'medium' likelihood values.
Likelihood \u00d7 impact risk rubric scoring market, execution, technology, and regulatory risks on a 1-5 scale each, with named owners + mitigation actions per top-quartile risk.
Extends a customer journey map with back-stage actors, support systems, and the line-of-visibility, producing a single artefact that ties UX friction to operational root causes.

Produces a single-interview fast-loop spec (30-min prep + 60-min interview + 60-min synthesis inside 36h, anchored to one decision) so single-shot interviews compound into evidence instead of evaporating.
Produces a success-metrics spec (1 north-star + \u22645 AARRR KPIs + targets + baselines + vanity-flagged exclusions) so the team measures what drives business outcomes, not what's easy to count.
Designs a bias-linted survey: <=12 questions, no leading/double-barreled/loaded wording, mandatory open-text for top 2 themes, sample-size N>=120 per segment for 90% CI +/-9%.
Generates a labelled synthetic-user response panel for directional hypothesis triage, always followed by real-user validation before any product decision.
Codes interview transcripts into themes via two-pass open + axial coding, with inter-coder reliability check, producing a tagged-insights report wired to a design backlog.
Multi-source signal aggregation (search interest + research papers + funding + GitHub stars + social) producing a trend score (0-100) and decay-curve estimate; rejects single-source narratives.
General-purpose trend-analysis report combining PEST/STEEP forces, named signals, hype-curve placement, and second-order implications with a 12-24 month horizon.
Produces a use-case spec (actor + goal + preconditions + main flow + alternative flows + postcondition per use-case) so engineering and QA share an unambiguous map of what users actually do.

Produces a user-interview report (Mom Test script + diarized transcripts + behavioural-ask outcome per session + frequency-counted insight list) so customer-discovery conversations produce evidence, not compliments.

9-stage AI-augmented research-ops pipeline (intake -> sampling -> instrumentation -> collection -> transcription -> coding -> synthesis -> review -> publish) with frozen codebook + N>=500/wk capacity + HITL gates.
Produces a forced-decision validation record (72h budget + ship-or-park verdict + named risk + falsification trigger) so indie hackers exit validation loops in days, not months.
Produces a value-proposition-canvas spec (customer profile jobs/pains/gains + value map relievers/creators + alignment-gap list) so positioning is anchored on evidence, not adjective stacking.
Quarterly win/loss interview program with 50/50 sample frame, neutral-interviewer rule, evidence-anchored script, and three-channel synthesis to PM/marketing/sales.

Workflow router that picks the correct end-to-end research workflow (market / competitors / pricing / personas / validation / pricing-research / names / pains) and emits a workflow plan with checkpoints.
Van Westendorp + Gabor-Granger questionnaire ready to run, with sample-frame rules, anchoring-bias controls, and a scoring sheet that emits an acceptable-price-range plus optimal-price-point report.
